segments.text: I loved him, And out of Egypt I called My son.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: As they called them, So they went from them;
They sacrificed to the Baals, And burned incense to carved images.
I taught Ephraim to walk, Taking them by their arms; But they did not know that I healed them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I drew them with gentle cords, With bands of love, And I was to them as those who take the yoke from their neck. I stooped and fed them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He shall not return to the land of Egypt; But the Assyrian shall be his king, Because they refused to repent.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And the sword shall slash in his cities, Devour his districts, And consume them, Because of their own counsels.
My people are bent on backsliding from Me. Though they call to the Most High, None at all exalt Him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, Israel? How can I make you like Admah? How can I set you like Zeboiim? My heart churns within Me; My sympathy is stirred.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will not execute the fierceness of My anger; I will not again destroy Ephraim. For I am God, and not man, The Holy One in your midst; And I will not come with terror.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They shall walk after the Lord. He will roar like a lion. When He roars, Then His sons shall come trembling from the west;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They shall come trembling like a bird from Egypt, Like a dove from the land of Assyria. And I will let them dwell in their houses,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Ephraim has encircled Me with lies, And the house of Israel with deceit; But Judah still walks with God, Even with the Holy One who is faithful.
segments.speaker: God
